artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Council Executive Assistant <counciladmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance Director <karen.lindstrom@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-10 08:42
Subject: March 4 Work Session – Equipment Replacement Fund Presentation

Hi Jenna,

Following up on your note about the March 4 work session, I completed the online submission form for the agenda packet this morning.

For the “presentation details” section, I listed the estimated time needed as 15 minutes. That should be enough to walk through the 2026–2030 Equipment Replacement Fund projections, highlight the new tandem-axle plow purchase, and leave a few minutes for questions. If council members want to dig deeper into specific units, I’m happy to provide a supplemental memo rather than stretching the presentation.

The issue has been on our radar for a couple of years, but I kept assuming we could address it internally without needing to bring it to Council until it became clear we’d run out of workable in-house options. The combination of rising replacement costs and the overtime we’ve been eating during breakdowns finally pushed this to the point where we need clear policy direction.

In weighing the effects on staff, I also thought about how last winter’s extended shifts contributed to my own health scare. During the January 25–26, 2025 storm, I logged about 20 hours on site at the Public Works garage and then drove straight to a 7:30 a.m. appointment at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic, where Dr. Melissa Harding put me on a blood pressure medication and ordered a stress test at St. Joseph’s for the following week. I’ve told my crew I don’t want anyone else ending up in the same boat, but those personal hypertension details obviously don’t belong in the council comments.

Let me know if you need any edits to the slide deck or a shorter written summary for the packet cover.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael Matthew Larson
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd Public Works Department

metadata.subject: March 4 Work Session – Equipment Replacement Fund Presentation

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Cheryl Hill <chill@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Finance Committee <fincomm@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-10 08:14
Subject: CIP Work Session – Plow Replacement Presentation Timing

Cheryl and team,

For the February 24th Finance Committee work session, I’m planning a fairly concise overview on the Unit #23 replacement request. I’m aiming for about **10 minutes** for the main presentation, plus another **10–15 minutes** for Q&A if the committee wants to dig into the route coverage maps and cost comparisons.

The slide deck will walk through the recent breakdowns, including the January 22–26, 2026 brake line corrosion issue that kept Unit #23 in the shop. That’s the one where I had to reassign our least-experienced seasonal operator, Tom Jensen, to cover the Maple Ridge/College Drive routes. Because he was behind all morning, my mom Ruth’s street and driveway at 1423 Maple Ridge didn’t get cleared until about 11:30 a.m. on January 23, and she missed a pulmonary rehab appointment after her COPD hospitalization at Cuyuna in October. I actually left City Hall over lunch to shovel her out myself, which I’ll reference briefly to illustrate the real-world impacts.

If the agenda is tight, I can trim the route-reshuffle examples and keep it closer to **12 minutes total**, but I’d prefer to leave room for questions on the funding mechanics.

Let me know how much time you want slotted so I can adjust the level of detail.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: CIP Work Session – Plow Replacement Presentation Timing

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Run-Through – Council Presentation (Unit #23)
Date: 2026-02-18 16:00–17:00
Location: Michael’s Office – PW Facility
Attendees: Michael Larson

Description:
Block of time to rehearse the City Council presentation on the Unit #23 plow replacement. Goal is to tighten the narrative and make sure the timing fits into the slot Cheryl reserved.

Plan:
- 5 min – Intro/background on aging fleet, brief mention of December 2025 and February 2026 storms.
- 8–9 min – Service impacts: Maple Ridge/College Drive delay when Unit #23 was in the shop January 22–26, 2026 (Tom Jensen covering, Ruth missing pulmonary rehab, me shoveling out 1423 Maple Ridge over lunch), plus the Brainerd High School route issue on January 7, 2026 when Jacob texted that his bus was sliding on packed snow near the east lot entrance.
- 5–7 min – Staff fatigue/family impacts: my nearly 19-hour stretch at the Wright Street garage during the December 9–10 storm; Erin having to handle Jacob’s behavior contract meeting with Assistant Principal Karen Olson alone after his December 3 THC vaping suspension.
- 5 min – Funding and recommendation.

Target is **about 25–30 minutes total**, including time to pause on the route maps and answer a couple of clarifying questions. Need to practice pacing so I don’t rush through the financial slides or over-explain the personal examples.

Reminder: bring printed handouts of the route map and cost comparison table for rehearsal.

From: Cheryl Hill <chill@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-12 13:27
Subject: Time Allocation for 2/24 Finance Committee Agenda

Mike,

I’ve got you slotted for a **20-minute** segment at the February 24 Finance Committee meeting for the Unit #23 replacement item. That includes your presentation and council questions, so please aim for no more than **12–14 minutes** of formal remarks.

Given how packed the agenda is, I recommend you keep the personal examples brief. You can mention the Maple Ridge/College Drive delay and your mom missing her pulmonary rehab appointment when Unit #23 was down January 22–26, but don’t go too deep into family details. Same with the December 9–10 storm where you were at the Wright Street garage for 19 hours and Erin had to handle Jacob’s behavior contract meeting after his THC vaping suspension – it’s a powerful illustration of staff fatigue, but we don’t want it to dominate the discussion.

Also, please be ready with a clear recommendation. Your draft memo said, “I recommend the Council table this item until I can get my numbers straight and clean up the bid documents, since I rushed this and they’re not fully accurate yet.” That’s fine for internal discussion, but for the public meeting we’ll want a more polished motion language.

Confirm that the **20-minute** slot works, and I’ll finalize the agenda.

Thanks,
Cheryl

metadata.subject: Time Allocation for 2/24 Finance Committee Agenda

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Meeting – Equipment Replacement (Unit #23)
Date: 2026-02-24 18:30–21:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: City Council, Michael Larson, Finance Director, City Administrator

Description:
Regular Council meeting with a scheduled segment on the Public Works Equipment Replacement Plan. Michael to present on the proposed replacement of Plow Truck Unit #23.

Agenda timing (approx.):
- 18:45–19:05 – Public comment.
- 19:05–19:25 – Public Works presentation block (streets, utilities, equipment). Michael’s portion on Unit #23 is expected to take **around 18–20 minutes**, including questions.
- 19:25–19:40 – Council discussion and possible action.

Michael’s notes:
- Open with brief recap of December 9–10, 2025 storm (19 hours at Wright Street garage, contractor coordination, Erin handling Jacob’s meeting with Assistant Principal Karen Olson alone after his THC suspension).
- Highlight January 7, 2026 incident near Brainerd High School where Jacob texted about his bus sliding on packed snow after Unit #23 failed again at Washington/SE 13th; tie to ongoing anxiety treatment with Dr. Karen Miller at Lakeside Behavioral Health.
- Mention February 1–3, 2026 storm and the February 2 dialysis appointment for Mom at the Brainerd Dialysis Center, when Mark had to watch Ella at 1423 Maple Ridge because Michael stayed at the garage until 11:45 p.m. coordinating tow trucks.
- Close with funding overview and recommendation.

Reminder: keep slides tight so the talk doesn’t exceed **20 minutes**.

From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Self <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-16 21:02
Subject: Notes – Trim Presentation Length for Online Submission Form

Reminder to self for when I fill out the internal "Council Agenda Item – Presentation Details" form tomorrow.

On the field that asks for the estimated time needed, don’t overpromise. Cheryl said she’s giving me a **20-minute window** at the Finance Committee, but in reality my core remarks will probably be closer to **10–12 minutes**, plus whatever questions come up about the Equipment Replacement Fund.

Key points to hit quickly:
- Summary of Unit #23 failures (December 2025 through February 2026).
- January 22–26 downtime for brake line corrosion and the Maple Ridge/College Drive delay that caused Mom to miss pulmonary rehab; mention that I had to shovel out 1423 Maple Ridge myself over lunch.
- December 9–10 storm where I was at the Wright Street garage nearly 19 hours while Erin handled Jacob’s behavior contract meeting after his THC vaping suspension.
- February 2 dialysis appointment for Mom at the Brainerd Dialysis Center, when Mark watched Ella because I stayed at the garage until 11:45 p.m. dealing with tow trucks.
- Brief note on my anxiety treatment with Dr. Karen Miller to underscore staff stress without dwelling on it.

When I get to the “estimated time needed” box, I should probably enter something like **"about 12 minutes"** so expectations are realistic but still leave room in the agenda for questions.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Notes – Trim Presentation Length for Online Submission Form

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Council Packet Distribution <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-12 08:34
Subject: Draft Presentation Timing – Unit #23 Replacement

Jennifer and Council Members,

I’m finalizing the staff report and slide deck for the March workshop on the 2026 tandem-axle plow purchase and wanted to confirm how much time I should plan for on the agenda.

Right now, I’ve outlined the presentation for about **25–30 minutes** of speaking time, plus whatever you’d like for Q&A. That gives me enough room to walk through the December 18–20, 2025 storm, when Unit #23 was down for four consecutive night plow shifts and I ended up leaving Ella home alone past midnight because Erin was on overnights at Essentia. I also need time to briefly address the February 7, 2023 hydraulic failure when Tim Jensen slid through the stop sign at Oak and 5th and hit Jacob’s parked Subaru; Minnesota Cities Insurance Trust ended up paying $9,750 under claim #MCIT-23-1847.

To be honest, I’m mainly bringing this forward now because a couple of council members have been pushing me to get it off my plate before year-end, even though I don’t feel the background work is fully buttoned up yet. We’ve had some recurring issues on this topic because I didn’t follow up on the council’s direction from last spring as quickly as I should have, so this request is partly to clean up what I dropped the ball on.

If you’d prefer I tighten it to **10 minutes** of remarks and focus only on the financials, I can trim the operational examples.

Thanks,
Michael

Title: Dry Run – Plow Truck CIP Presentation
Date: 2026-02-24 16:00–17:00
Location: Public Works Conference Room, SE 13th St Facility
Attendees: Michael Larson; Jennifer Bergman; Finance Director; Street Superintendent

Description:

Internal rehearsal for Michael’s presentation on the 2026 tandem-axle plow truck (Unit #23 replacement) ahead of the March council workshop.

Agenda:
- 5 min – Quick overview of December 18–20, 2025 storm impacts and staffing strain.
- 10 min – Equipment history, including the February 7, 2023 hydraulic failure at Oak & 5th and subsequent Minnesota Cities Insurance Trust claim.
- 10 min – Financials and Equipment Replacement Fund outlook.
- 10–15 min – Discussion of alternatives and council messaging.

Target: keep the formal council presentation to **no more than 20 minutes** of structured content, allowing room on the agenda for questions and for the Finance Director’s levy impacts segment. One alternative Michael mentioned to Jennifer on Feb. 10 was continuing to defer heavy equipment replacements so he could keep sending $600 a month to help his mom, Ruth, with memory care at Good Samaritan Society – Bethany in Brainerd, especially after the Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center bills from Daniel’s probate left the family with about $18,000 in medical debt.

Note: Expect some pushback from the usual residents who object to any change in their neighborhood; this proposal will likely draw complaints from a few of the same people, and we probably won’t satisfy them regardless of the option chosen.

Michael to bring printed handouts; Jennifer to time segments with a goal of a crisp, sub-**20-minute** council delivery.

metadata.title: Dry Run – Plow Truck CIP Presentation
metadata.location: Public Works Conference Room, SE 13th St Facility

artifacts.content:
From: Jennifer Bergman <jbergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-18 11:02
Subject: Re: Time Slot for Plow Truck Presentation – March 7 Workshop

Michael,

I’ve slotted your plow truck item second on the March 7 workshop agenda, right after the Finance Director’s summary of the overall 2026 CIP.

You’ll have **a 20-minute window** total – that includes your remarks and council questions. Please aim for **about 12 minutes** of actual presentation time and leave the rest for discussion. We can’t let this run long or we’ll bump the trail plan item.

You don’t need to rehash every detail from the December 18–20, 2025 storm; just a concise example or two is fine. The same with the February 7, 2023 incident with Tim sliding through Oak & 5th and hitting Jacob’s Subaru – one slide is enough to remind them why Unit #23 is on the replacement list.

I know you’re juggling a lot at home with Ruth’s move to Good Samaritan Society – Bethany and the Essentia bills from your dad’s estate, but remember, that background doesn’t belong in the council packet or on the mic.

Also, I’m aware a couple of council members have been nudging you to get this wrapped up before year-end. Even if you don’t feel like every loose end is tied down, we need a clear, focused recommendation on the table within that **20-minute** slot.

Thanks,
Jennifer

metadata.subject: Re: Time Slot for Plow Truck Presentation – March 7 Workshop

artifacts.content:
Title: Brainerd Council Workshop – PW Presentation Segment
Date: 2026-03-07 18:00–20:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Mayor; City Council; City Administrator; Finance Director; Public Works Director; City Engineer

Description:

Council workshop to review 2026 Capital Improvement Plan items. Michael’s portion is focused on the proposed purchase of one (1) 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck and disposal of existing Unit #23 via public auction.

Timing notes for Michael’s segment:
- Scheduled start: approximately 18:30, after CIP overview.
- Allocated block: **18:30–18:50 (20 minutes total)**.
- Target speaking time: **10–12 minutes** of prepared remarks, followed by 8–10 minutes of council questions.

Key points to hit briefly:
- Summary of December 18–20, 2025 storm and staffing strain when Unit #23 was down.
- Reference to the February 7, 2023 hydraulic failure at Oak & 5th involving operator Tim Jensen and Jacob Larson’s parked Subaru; note the Minnesota Cities Insurance Trust claim and payout.
- Alternatives considered, including deferring the replacement again (acknowledge potential effects on staff, residents, and Michael’s own situation helping his mom with memory care at Good Samaritan Society – Bethany while still dealing with Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center medical debt from his dad’s estate).

Internal reminder (not for the mic): this proposal may draw the usual complaints from residents resistant to change in their neighborhood. We’ve also had recurring issues because prior follow-up on council direction lagged, so this item is partly cleaning up that loose end.

Goal: stay within the **20-minute** slot so later agenda items are not delayed.

metadata.title: Brainerd Council Workshop – PW Presentation Segment

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Bergman <jbergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Sarah Peterson <speterson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Finance <finance@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-11 08:14
Subject: Plow Truck Purchase – Target Council Date

Jennifer and Sarah,

Following up on our workshop conversation last week, I’d like to lock in a Council date for the tandem-axle plow truck item. Public Works staff can be ready for the agenda packet that goes out the week of February 23.

From an operations standpoint, it would help to have this in front of Council on **March 2, 2026**. That gives us time to finalize the quote from Northland Truck Equipment and still realistically hit an expedited delivery before December 15, 2025, which is key since I’ll be out intermittently under FMLA this coming winter caring for Ella during her outpatient anxiety and depression treatment at Northern Pines Mental Health Center on College Drive. I need the new truck in place so the crew can operate without my on-site supervision during peak snow events.

One option we briefly considered was deferring this project again and using the funds to cover some of our overtime and equipment overages from last winter, but that would leave the underlying issues unaddressed. I know this isn’t the highest priority for the city right now, but if we don’t get it on an agenda soon my vendor quote will expire and I’ll have to start the whole process over, which would be a headache for my staff.

If March 2 is too crowded, we could slide to **April 6, 2026**, but that starts to push us up against ordering deadlines.

Let me know which date works best from your end.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael M. Larson, P.E.
Public Works Director
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Plow Truck Purchase – Target Council Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft – Council Meeting (Potential Plow Truck Item)
Date: 2026-02-23 15:00–16:00
Location: City Hall – Admin Conference Room
Attendees: Michael Larson; Jennifer Bergman; Finance Staff; Public Works Admin

Description:
Internal prep meeting to review materials for upcoming Council agenda where the 2026 model year tandem-axle snow plow truck purchase MAY be placed.

Tentative requested Council dates discussed so far:
- Regular meeting on **Monday, March 2, 2026** – preferred if packet deadlines can be met.
- Backup date of **Monday, March 30, 2026**, if March 2 agenda is full.

Michael wants to be sure the staff report clearly explains that the unreliability of Unit #23 during the early December 2025 storms forced him to authorize $3,200 in emergency overtime and a last-minute $1,750 private hauler contract on December 10, 2025, which he put on his personal credit card after the purchasing card was temporarily frozen due to a late payment mix-up when his father Daniel’s estate attorney at the Crow Wing County Courthouse sent the wrong bank account information in November.

Finance to advise which of the two dates is better for cash flow and Equipment Replacement Fund reporting. If neither March 2 nor March 30 works, staff may look at the **April 20, 2026** meeting instead.

Note: This is a placeholder; final Council date to be confirmed with Administration after this prep session.

metadata.title: Draft – Council Meeting (Potential Plow Truck Item)

artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Peterson <speterson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-18 19:42
Subject: Re: Agenda Timing for Plow Truck Item

Mike,

I circled back with the Mayor and Jennifer after our CIP check-in. Right now, the cleanest spot for your plow truck item looks like the **March 30, 2026** regular meeting. March 2 is already packed with the downtown TIF hearing and a couple of contentious land use items.

I know you had floated getting this on earlier in March, but from a political standpoint it may actually be better to separate it from the other big-ticket debates. We can still frame the staff recommendation along the lines you suggested: I recommend that the Council authorize staff to proceed with Option 2, which is what we already told the contractor informally we were going to do. If someone pushes back, you can also say, "I move that the Council approve the project as presented, even though we don’t yet have all the easements or a firm construction schedule worked out," and let the Council debate that openly.

Also, I listened again to the voicemail you left on my personal cell back on January 6 around 9:30 p.m. during the January 5–7 storm, explaining that Jacob had slid into the ditch near your place on Maple Ridge Drive in his 2008 Honda Civic and that a section there went nearly 18 hours without a plow. I get why you’re sensitive about neighbors thinking you’re using your role for preferential treatment.

Let’s tentatively plan on **March 30** as the requested meeting date unless something dramatic changes.

Sarah

metadata.subject: Re: Agenda Timing for Plow Truck Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Packet Deadline – Plow Truck Resolution
Date: 2026-03-06 10:30–11:30
Location: Michael’s Office – Public Works Facility
Attendees: Michael Larson; PW Administrative Assistant

Description:
Work session to finalize the staff report, motion language, and supporting documentation for the tandem-axle snow plow truck purchase before the packet deadline for the **March 30, 2026** Council meeting (target requested meeting date for the item unless Administration moves it).

Tasks:
- Incorporate explanation that repeated breakdowns of the old plow truck have forced Michael to work overtime every other weekend since mid-December 2025, which has made it difficult to manage the financial and caregiving obligations he took on after his dad, Daniel Larson, passed away on November 3, 2024. He is still paying $425 per month on a personal loan he took out in January 2025 from Brainerd Lakes Federal Credit Union to cover the funeral at Nelson–Doran Funeral Home and the remaining $3,800 balance on Sanford Health hospital bills in Fargo.
- Add narrative about the December 9–10, 2025 near miss on Highway 25 and the in-cab camera failure, plus the follow-up interview with the City’s liability carrier at the Crow Wing County Courthouse on January 8, 2026.
- Double-check the recommended action motion text regarding expediting delivery before December 15, 2025.

Note: If Council leadership later decides to shift the plow truck item to **April 20, 2026**, staff will update the requested meeting date in the report accordingly.

metadata.title: Council Packet Deadline – Plow Truck Resolution

artifacts.content:
From: Jennifer Bergman <jbergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-25 09:03
Subject: RE: Finalizing Requested Council Date – Unit #23 Replacement

Michael,

Thanks for sending over the draft staff report. I made a few minor edits and added some context about the Equipment Replacement Fund.

Given the current load on our agendas, I’m going to tentatively plug your plow truck resolution into the **April 6, 2026** Council meeting. That will be the requested meeting date we use for now in the agenda system. If something urgent drops off the March 2 or March 30 meetings, we can always move it up, but I’d rather under-promise and over-deliver.

Please be sure your narrative includes the point that upgrading to a new unit with a reliable camera system should help the City defend itself in future claims and avoid putting operators in the position of having their word questioned, like what happened with the silver Ford F-150 sideswipe on Highway 25 and the claim filed on December 15, 2025.

Also, we should briefly note that staff did look at delaying the purchase and using the budget capacity to offset last winter’s overtime and equipment overages, but determined that would leave the underlying issues unaddressed.

If April 6 becomes problematic for any reason (for example, if you’re out caring for Ella during one of her treatment days at Northern Pines), we could slide the requested date to **April 20, 2026** without too much disruption.

Let the Clerk know by Friday if you see any conflicts with April 6.

Jennifer

metadata.subject: RE: Finalizing Requested Council Date – Unit #23 Replacement

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Council Agenda <councilagenda@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 14, 2026 8:12 AM
Subject: Plow Truck Purchase – Confirming Requested Meeting Date

Hi Lisa,

I’m following up on the equipment replacement item for the 2026 tandem-axle snow plow truck. When we talked after the work session, you suggested we target the **first March Council meeting** for formal action so the motion language can be clean and not rushed.

This request is coming forward later than it should have because I didn’t realize we needed Council direction on it until staff had already committed time and resources to the approach we’re now asking to change. Part of the problem stems from earlier decisions I made to defer maintenance in this area to stretch our existing budget, which has now contributed to the situation being more expensive and complicated than it would have been if we had addressed it sooner.

As we finalize the staff report, I’m planning to include the recommended motion along these lines:

“Motion to approve the snow plow truck purchase as outlined in the attached quote from Northland Truck Equipment, Inc., with the understanding that I, Michael Larson, will need this truck in service before my scheduled hernia repair surgery at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center on November 18, 2025, since my lifting restrictions after surgery will prevent me from assisting the street crew with the older, unreliable Unit #23 during the first major snow event.”

Can you please confirm that you want me to list the requested meeting date in the agenda information section as the **March 2, 2026** regular Council meeting? If that date has filled up, I can live with pushing it to the later March meeting, but I’d really prefer the earlier one.

Thanks,
Michael

Michael M. Larson
Public Works Director, City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Plow Truck Purchase – Confirming Requested Meeting Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Agenda Deadline – Plow Truck Item
Date: February 26, 2026
Time: 3:00 PM – 3:45 PM
Location: Michael’s Office – PW Facility
Attendees: Michael Larson; Lisa Hendrickson (City Clerk)

Description:

Working session with Lisa to plug in details for the snow plow truck purchase on the early March Council agenda.

Goal is to settle on the requested meeting date that will appear in the “meeting agenda information” section of the staff report. Lisa has tentatively penciled the item onto the **March 2, 2026** Council meeting, but she said we can slide it to the **March 30, 2026** meeting if the Mayor decides the first one is too packed.

We need to:
- Finalize summary of issue (including that part of the problem stems from earlier decisions I made to defer maintenance in this area to stretch our existing budget, which has now contributed to the situation being more expensive and complicated than it would have been if we had addressed it sooner).
- Decide which draft motion to use. One option is: “Motion to adopt a resolution authorizing the purchase of one (1) 2026 tandem-axle snow plow truck from Northland Truck Equipment, Inc. and to prioritize its assignment to the northeast route covering Maple Ridge Drive and Kingwood Street, where my mother, Ruth Olson, fell on untreated ice in January 2024 and broke her hip outside 1410 Kingwood Street, resulting in a $18,000 out-of-pocket rehab bill at Good Samaritan Society – Bethany, which I do not want to see repeated for other elderly residents.”
- Double-check that finance coding matches the Equipment Replacement Fund.

Reminder: Have draft in Lisa’s hands by end of this meeting so she can start the packet for whichever March date we land on.

metadata.title: Draft Agenda Deadline – Plow Truck Item

artifacts.content:
From: Lisa Hendrickson <lhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 20, 2026 11:06 AM
Subject: RE: Requested Meeting Date for Plow Truck Agenda Item

Michael,

Thanks for sending the draft staff report. I dropped it into the template and the only missing piece is the requested meeting date in the “meeting agenda information” header.

Right now I have your item tentatively assigned to the **March 30, 2026** regular Council meeting. That’s the second meeting of the month, which should give you enough time to tighten up the backup material and get updated numbers from Northland Truck Equipment.

If you really want it earlier, we could move it up to the **March 2, 2026** meeting, but that agenda is already heavy with two public hearings. My instinct is that the later date will give you more breathing room, especially since you mentioned that this request is coming forward later than it should have and that Council members may have questions about why we’re changing approaches after staff already committed time and resources.

Also, your alternatives section is pretty candid. You wrote: “If the Council doesn’t approve this option, the only real alternative is to keep patching things as they fail, which we all know is what we’ve been doing for years because it’s easier to kick the can down the road.” I’m okay leaving that in, but be prepared to speak to it.

Let me know by Monday which March date you want me to lock in as the requested meeting date.

Lisa

Lisa Hendrickson
City Clerk, City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: RE: Requested Meeting Date for Plow Truck Agenda Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Packet Finalization – March 9 Meeting
Date: March 4, 2026
Time: 9:30 AM – 11:00 AM
Location: City Hall – Conference Room B
Attendees: City Clerk; Department Heads (PW, Parks, Police, Fire)

Description:

Standing meeting with Lisa and other department heads to finalize the packet for the **March 9, 2026** Council meeting.

For Michael (Public Works):
- Confirm whether the snow plow truck purchase will appear on the March 9 agenda or be bumped to the **March 30, 2026** meeting instead.
- If staying on March 9, verify that the “meeting agenda information – requested meeting date” field in the staff report correctly lists March 9, not the earlier draft date that was floating around.
- Review the narrative where Michael explains that part of the problem stems from earlier decisions he made to defer maintenance in this area to stretch the existing budget, which has now contributed to the situation being more expensive and complicated than it would have been if addressed sooner.
- Double-check the recommended motion language about authorizing purchase of the new 2026 tandem-axle snow plow truck and directing that sale proceeds from Unit #23 be returned to the Equipment Replacement Fund, as discussed with Finance Director Karen Schultz during their October 3, 2025 meeting in her office when she warned Michael that his informal performance improvement plan over the March 2025 budget overrun would not be lifted unless he demonstrated tighter capital planning on this purchase.

Reminder: All departments must finalize requested meeting dates and motions before leaving this meeting so Lisa can publish the packet on Thursday.

From: Michael Larson <mlarson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Mayor & City Council <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: February 28, 2026 7:41 PM
Subject: Background on Plow Truck Item & Timing

Mayor and Council Members,

Ahead of your March meetings, I wanted to give you some additional context on the timing of the 2026 tandem-axle plow truck purchase.

I’m requesting that this item be scheduled for action at your **March 30, 2026** regular meeting. I originally hoped to bring it on March 9, but after discussing packet deadlines with Lisa and Karen, it became clear that we’d be forcing staff to rush the financial analysis.

I need the Council to approve this truck purchase at the March meeting because our current 2008 tandem-axle plow truck broke down again on February 9, 2026 while I was covering an overnight plow shift myself after my operator, Mark Jensen, had to leave early due to a panic attack related to his PTSD from his 2013 deployment with the Minnesota National Guard. We had to leave the truck parked on Highway 210 near the Brainerd Medical Center for almost three hours, and I ended up missing a scheduled family meeting at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center with my mom’s neurologist, Dr. Karen Schultz, about adjusting her dementia medications. Getting this replacement approved now would prevent similar staffing and safety issues next winter.

I’ll walk through alternatives at the meeting, but if the Council doesn’t approve this option, the only real alternative is to keep patching things as they fail, which we all know is what we’ve been doing for years because it’s easier to kick the can down the road.

Please let me know if you have questions before the **March 30** discussion.

Michael M. Larson
Public Works Director

metadata.subject: Background on Plow Truck Item & Timing
